free_text: Cross section measurements of alpha- induced reactions
on Mo-92,94 and Sn-112 for p-process studies.
The sample material was deposited on 0.4 mm thick Ta
backings. The Mo sample were produced by sputtering
natural metallic Mo in a pure argon atmosphere.
These samples were 10 mm in diameter and exhibited an
area density between 5*E+17 and 3*E+18 Mo atoms/cm**2,
corresponding to layer thicknesses between 100 and
The Sn-112 samples were prepared from 10 mg of enriched
Correction for gamma cascade and X-rays was made.
*By compiler*. En in data-section is the effective
alpha-energy. The error bars of E-eff is asymmetric.
En-cm gives the effective beam energy(< beam energy)
at which 50% of the total yields is obtained.
